title: Hurricane Uncertainty Guidance
content: Wide confidence intervals mean planners should prepare primary and adjacent coastal zones, pre-stage communications, and update evacuation triggers as track certainty improves.

title: Flood Response Planning Note
content: When rainfall totals exceed drainage thresholds and river levels are rising, planners should inspect levees, prioritize floodplain populations, and prepare shelter transportation.

title: Humanitarian Impact Checklist
content: High displacement risk and fragile water access increase the need for shelter, sanitation, and child protection coordination in the first operational period.

title: Infrastructure Consequence Brief
content: If hospitals, bridges, or airport corridors are exposed, planners should emphasize continuity operations, patient transfer options, and alternate logistics routes.

title: Evacuation Capacity Reference
content: Low transport capacity or expected road congestion means phased evacuation messaging and contraflow decision points should be prepared earlier than usual.
